creator: ino-news@spotteswoode.dnsalias.org
text:
> clemens fischer:

> > dougb@FreeBSD.org:
> 
> > Synopsis: ports-mgmt/portmaster [feature request]: naming log files, hooks
> > 
> > State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> > State-Changed-By: dougb
> > State-Changed-When: Fri Nov 7 21:05:56 UTC 2008
> > State-Changed-Why: 
> > 
> > Hooks are a non-starter at this point as I have no time for it.
> 
> I'll give this topic some thought, maybe there's something simple that
> is safe and easy to implement.  There are points in your really nifty
> script where information is available that could be used eg. setting
> variables I need in my wrapper.  That wrapper duplicates some of the
> work portmaster does just to get at the name of the port category and
> the port name, only to construct a name for a log file!

I have the following patch implementing a starting point for hooks.  It
provides what I need most: named logfiles.  The changes are meant to be
non-intrusive and safe.  If the user doesn't supply hooks or they don't
work, portmaster doesn't change its behaviour.  The patch is appended.
My hooks file looks like this:

  # portmaster-hooks
  __user_logdir="/var/pkgtools"
  __user_category=""
  __user_portname=""
  mk_log_filename()
  {
      local prefix="${1:-port_log}"
      local pid="${2}"
      local portdir="${3}"
      __user_category="${portdir%/*}"
      __user_portname="${portdir#*/}"
      echo "${__user_logdir}/${__user_category}__${__user_portname}.txt"
      return 0
  }
  dispose_log_file()
  {
      return 0
  }


regards, clemens

--- /l/sbin/portmaster	2008-08-03 00:26:38.000000000 +0200
+++ /root/bin/portmaster.sh	2008-12-22 17:05:54.000000000 +0100
@@ -33,10 +33,17 @@
 	# If we are already root, unset this to avoid potential conflict
 	euid=`ps -o uid $$` ; euid=${euid##* }
 	[ "$euid" -eq 0 ] && unset PM_SU_CMD
 fi
 
+# Get user-hooks.  If put into an RC file, they do not work in children.
+# Read global hooks file first
+[ -r /etc/portmaster-hooks ] && . /etc/portmaster-hooks
+[ -r /usr/local/etc/portmaster-hooks ] && . /usr/local/etc/portmaster-hooks
+# Read local hooks next
+[ -r "$HOME/.portmaster-hooks" ] && . $HOME/.portmaster-hooks
+
 umask 022
 
 #=============== Begin functions we always want to have ===============
 
 version () {
@@ -78,10 +85,11 @@
 			n=$(( $n + 1 ))
 			kill_bad_children
 		done
 		if [ -n "$HIDE_BUILD" ]; then
 			local logs file
+            # FIXME! -ino: path hardcoded
 			logs=`echo ${TMPDIR}/port_log-${PARENT_PID}-*`
 			case "$logs" in *\*) unset logs ;; esac
 			if [ -n "$logs" ]; then
 				echo ''
 				echo "===>>> Build/Install logs available:"
@@ -319,10 +327,46 @@
 pm_pkg_delete_s   () { $PM_SU_CMD /usr/sbin/pkg_delete $*; }
 pm_rm_s           () { $PM_SU_CMD /bin/rm $*; }
 pm_rmdir_s        () { $PM_SU_CMD /bin/rmdir $*; }
 pm_unlink_s       () { /bin/test -e $1 && $PM_SU_CMD /bin/unlink $1; }
 
+# Functions used for implementing user-hooks
+
+# Check for user-defined function
+function_defined()
+{
+    local name="${1}"
+    local typ="$(type "${name}" 2>/dev/null || echo '__nothing__')"
+    local ex=1
+    case ".${typ}" in
+        .__nothing__) ex=1;;
+        *is*/*) ex=1;;
+        *'an alias'*) ex=1;;
+        *'shell function'*) ex=0;;
+        *'shell builtin'*) ex=1;;
+        *) ex=1;;
+    esac
+    return $ex
+}
+
+# Try to make sure a filename is reasonably safe to use
+check_filename()
+{
+    local name="${1}"
+    local ex=1
+    case ".${name}" in
+        */boot/*|*/bin/*|*/sbin/*|*/lib/*|*/libexec/*) ex=1;;
+        ./*/*) ex=0;;
+        *) ex=1;;
+    esac
+    [ $ex -ne 0 ] && return 1
+    [ -x "${name}" -o -d "${name}" -o -p "${name}" ] && ex=1
+    [ -L "${name}" -o -S "${name}" ] && ex=1
+    [ -O "${name}" -o -G "${name}" ] || ex=1
+    return $ex
+}
+
 #=============== End functions we always want to have ===============
 
 # Do this here so it can use the fancy functions above, and default values
 # can be overridden in the rc files
 if [ "$$" -eq "$PARENT_PID" ]; then
@@ -2088,10 +2132,13 @@
 	fi
 done
 unset sleep
 
 if [ -n "$HIDE_BUILD" ]; then
+    function_defined "mk_log_filename" &&
+	port_log=`mk_log_filename port_log ${PARENT_PID} ${portdir}`
+    check_filename "${port_log}" ||
 	port_log=`mktemp -t port_log-${PARENT_PID}-${portdir#*/}`
 	port_log_args=">> $port_log 2>&1"
 	echo "===>>> Logging build to $port_log"
 fi
 
@@ -2312,11 +2359,15 @@
 
 INSTALLED_LIST="${INSTALLED_LIST}\t${ilist}\n"
 [ -e "$pdb/$new_port/+DISPLAY" ] && DISPLAY_LIST="${DISPLAY_LIST}$new_port "
 CUR_DEPS="${CUR_DEPS}${new_port}:${portdir}:"
 
-[ -n "$HIDE_BUILD" -a -n "$port_log" ] && pm_unlink $port_log
+if [ -n "$HIDE_BUILD" -a -n "$port_log" ]; then
+    function_defined "dispose_log_file" &&
+    dispose_log_file $port_log ||
+    pm_unlink $port_log
+fi
 
 [ -n "$URB_YES" -o -n "$UPDATE_REQ_BYS" ] &&
 	URB_DONE_LIST="${URB_DONE_LIST}${new_port}:"
 [ -n "$URB_YES" -o -n "$UPDATE_REQ_BYS" -o -n "$PM_FORCE" ] &&
 	$PM_SU_CMD touch $pdb/$new_port/PM_UPGRADE_DONE_FLAG
